title: Deploy app, configure secrets & smoke test
description: Get Unpack running on EC2 and verify everything works. Store env vars securely in AWS SSM Parameter Store (not hardcoded .env). Write a reusable deploy script that pulls secrets, builds the app, and restarts PM2. Clone the repo, deploy, update OAuth redirect URIs, and run a full smoke test.

plan.text: Store 4 env vars in AWS SSM Parameter Store as SecureStrings: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, DATABASE_URL, VITE_SUPABASE_URL, VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY.
Write deploy.sh script: pulls SSM params into .env, runs git pull, bun install, bun run build, pm2 restart. Make executable.
acceptance_criteria: Pulls all 4 env vars from SSM

plan.text: Clone repo on EC2, run deploy script, verify app starts on port 3000 and is accessible via Elastic IP on port 80.
acceptance_criteria: `pm2 logs` shows app running without errors

plan.text: Update Supabase redirect URIs and Google OAuth origins/redirect URIs with the Elastic IP address.
acceptance_criteria: Supabase dashboard has http://<elastic-ip> as allowed redirect
Google Cloud Console has the IP in authorized origins + redirect URIs

plan.text: Full smoke test: OAuth login, send streaming message, verify conversation persists, check CloudWatch for logs. Set up CloudWatch agent if needed.
acceptance_criteria: OAuth login works at public URL
Env vars stored securely in SSM Parameter Store (not hardcoded)
No API keys exposed to client

context.notes: Shaky skills: Deployment, Security. The VITE_ prefixed vars get bundled into client JS at build time — they must be in .env BEFORE `bun run build`. DATABASE_URL and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY are server-only (read at runtime). Deploy script must handle this ordering. Step 4 is manual (Supabase dashboard + Google Cloud Console).
